headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Palantir Technologies (PLTR) After A 15x Three-Year Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether Palantir Technologies' current share price lines up with its underlying worth, you are not alone. This article is going to focus squarely on what that price might imply about value. The stock last closed at US$145.17, with returns of 12.7% over the past week, a 1% decline over the past month, a 13.5% decline year to date, a 72% gain over the last year, and a very large gain over three years that is more than 15x, alongside a strong multi year gain over five...

headline: Don't underestimate the role of Palantir, AI in Iran conflict
summary: Defense stocks — like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC) — are sliding in Tuesday's session amid escalations in the Middle East conflict after Israel bombed Iran and Lebanon. Baird Tech Strategist Ted Mortonson comes on Market Domination to discuss the role Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and other AI operators could have in Iran. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Pentagon dispute bolsters Anthropic reputation but raises questions about AI readiness in military
summary: Anthropic's moral stand on U.S. military use of artificial intelligence is reshaping the competition between leading AI companies but also exposing a growing awareness that maybe chatbots just aren't capable enough for acts of war.  Anthropic's chatbot Claude, for the first time, outpaced rival ChatGPT in phone app downloads in the United States this week, a signal of growing interest from consumers siding with Anthropic in its standoff with the Pentagon, according to market research firm Sensor Tower.  The Trump administration on Friday ordered government agencies to stop using Claude and designated it a supply chain risk after Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei refused to bend his company's ethical safeguards preventing the technology from being applied to autonomous weapons and domestic mass surveillance.

raw.headline: Pentagon dispute bolsters Anthropic reputation but raises questions about AI readiness in military

The Trump administration on Friday ordered government agencies to stop using Claude and designated it a supply chain risk after Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei refused to bend his company's ethical safeguards preventing the technology from being applied to autonomous weapons and domestic mass surveillance.

headline: Palantir Is Back on Wall Street’s Buy List After 38% Plunge
summary: The rally started last week as President Donald Trump ratcheted up threats against Iran, and is continuing amid military strikes by the US and Israel on the country.  “The positive move in the stock is an emotional reaction to how Palantir is positioned with the government and military,” said Tim Pagliara, chief investment officer at Capwealth Advisors, which owns the company’s shares.  “I don’t think the war represents a meaningful change to Palantir’s fundamentals, but it validates Palantir’s position within the government, and it positions them for continued growth and adoption in many other areas of the military,” he said.

raw.headline: Palantir Is Back on Wall Street’s Buy List After 38% Plunge
